data.title: Local workflow simplification: remove enforcement hooks and shift to PR-based merge
data.description:
## Context

With CI enforcement established by Epic 3aaa-0238 (CI-enforced quality gates: staged CI pipeline with LLM review and GitHub Ruleset enforcement), the local pre-commit and PreToolUse hooks that enforce test gate, review gate, and bypass detection are redundant. These hooks add friction to every commit — test-gate-status files, review-status files, diff hash verification, Layer 2 bypass detection — without providing enforcement value beyond what CI now guarantees. Removing them simplifies the local commit workflow and eliminates the timeout workarounds for test recording. The agent merge-to-main workflow must also transition from direct push to a PR-based workflow (gh pr create, poll CI, gh pr merge with auto flag) to comply with the Ruleset PR requirement.

## Success Criteria

- The local pre-commit test gate hook (pre-commit-test-gate.sh), review gate hook (pre-commit-review-gate.sh), and their entries in .pre-commit-config.yaml are removed in lockstep (same commit)
- The PreToolUse hooks that enforce no-verify restrictions (review-gate.sh), review-status files, and test-gate-status files are removed — CI enforcement via GitHub Rulesets replaces their function
- The local commit workflow retains tests, lint, and format as non-blocking Bash steps within the workflow (run before PR creation for fast feedback; failures surface as warnings, not commit blockers)
- A revised merge-to-main.sh (or replacement script) implements the PR-based workflow: create PR via gh pr create, poll CI status via gh pr checks or gh run watch, merge via gh pr merge with auto flag, and update the local worktree to origin/main after merge
- The COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md is updated to reflect the simplified local workflow (no review gate, no test gate, no LLM review step, non-blocking fast-feedback only)
- Existing worktree sessions that began before the hook removal receive a clear error message if they attempt the old merge-to-main.sh direct-push path, directing them to create a PR instead

## Dependencies

- 3aaa-0238 (CI-enforced quality gates) must be completed and verified — CI enforcement must be operational before local hooks are removed, to avoid a window without enforcement

## Approach

Sequential migration: verify Epic A CI enforcement is green for at least one full PR cycle, then remove local enforcement hooks and rewrite merge-to-main.sh for PR-based workflow. Hook scripts and .pre-commit-config.yaml entries are removed in the same commit to avoid hook-not-found errors. The commit workflow (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md) is simplified to: run tests/lint/format for feedback, commit, push, create PR, wait for CI, merge. PreToolUse hooks (review-gate.sh) and associated artifact files (review-status, test-gate-status, reviewer-findings.json) are removed.

## Scenario Analysis

- Hook removal residue in .pre-commit-config.yaml (high): hook scripts and config entries removed in lockstep (same commit)
- In-flight sessions break on cutover (high): existing merge-to-main.sh sessions get 403 after Ruleset activation; revised script detects and emits clear error directing to PR workflow
- PR merge race (high): agent merge call may fail if branch HEAD changes; merge workflow handles 422 with retry-after-repoll
- gh pr merge auto edge cases (high): documented failure modes in cli/cli issues 8206, 8514; merge workflow implements explicit error detection and retry
